_comment: Accessibility baseline — suppressed violations are explicitly accepted as known issues. Each rule-id key maps to a list of accepted node-target selectors. Entries prefixed with 're:' are regex patterns (used for Splunk-bundled DOM whose IDs are auto-generated per render). Every suppression MUST cite (1) the violation pattern, (2) the Splunk-specific reason it is acceptable, (3) the date added.
_aria-allowed-attr-rationale: (1) input[placeholder='Find'] is Splunk's global Find input in the dashboard header (role='textbox' + aria-expanded + aria-activedescendant — combination unsupported by axe but emitted by Splunk's bundled MVC). (2) The Splunk SimpleXML paginator widget renders N — axe flags aria-selected as disallowed on role='button'. Splunk-bundled DOM, upstream fix required. We accept BOTH selector forms axe-core emits for this widget: the fully-qualified '#view_NNN-paginatorNNN > .selected[aria-label="Selected page N"][aria-current="page"]' (used when there are multiple .selected on the page) and the simplified '.selected' (used when .selected is unique — observed on the audit dashboard whose paginator is the only .selected element at scan time). Added 2026-05-12 (Ring-5 Day-4 first-run triage); '.selected' added 2026-05-18 (Phase 2.15 second pass, CI run 26005972758).
_aria-valid-attr-value-rationale: Same Splunk global Find input — aria-activedescendant points to an internal Splunk widget ID that doesn't exist as a DOM element axe can verify. Splunk's bundled component, not ours. Added 2026-05-12.
_region-rationale: The notification badge is injected by appserver/static/notifications.js into Splunk's chrome header. There is no Splunk-provided landmark wrapper at that insertion point and wrapping the badge in our own <aside role='complementary'> would visually drift from Splunk's chrome layout. Acceptable to leave — the badge has aria-label and is keyboard reachable. Added 2026-05-12.
_aria-progressbar-name-rationale: Splunk's SimpleXML framework emits transient <div class='progress-animation' role='progressbar' id='viewNNNNN'> elements while panels search the audit index. The progressbar has aria-valuemin/max/now plus a meaningful aria-valuetext ('0.0% of the time range scanned.', 'Parsing job...') but no aria-label / aria-labelledby — those are not exposed by the Splunk MVC progressbar component, and the IDs are auto-generated per render. The audit dashboard surfaces this more than whitelist_manager/control_panel because its searches are heavier (audit index lookback) so panels are in the searching state at scan time. Fixing in our XML is not possible (Splunk framework emits the DOM); a MutationObserver shim to inject aria-label is queued for the v1.1 a11y sweep alongside the P2 span clear-button work. Same posture as the 3 prior Splunk-framework suppressions above. Added 2026-05-18 (Phase 2.15).
_color-contrast-rationale: The Splunk dev-license header badge — DevLicense: — renders white #ffffff text on orange #f1813f at 2.64:1 (WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text). It appears on EVERY page of a dev-license Splunk install (the badge is part of Splunk's chrome, not wl_manager). On a production Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud install with a real license, this badge does not exist at all — so the violation only fires in CI where we use a dev-license container (we don't pay for an Enterprise license for testing). The CSS-module class suffix ('20R1L') is a Splunk build hash that varies per Splunk version; using a regex anchored to the deterministic prefix 'devTest---pages-dark---dev---' makes the suppression stable across Splunk upgrades while staying narrow enough to NOT mask any real wl_manager contrast bug (no wl_ class shares this pattern). All 3 dashboard reports (whitelist_manager, control_panel, audit) emit the same identical violation — same root cause, single suppression closes all 3. Fixing in upstream Splunk is the only real remediation path; we can't restyle Splunk-framework DOM without overriding their chrome. Added 2026-05-20 (Phase 2.15 closure).
